threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think the bus will be on time. Real locals know it’s all tea and patience, especially on a Saturday morning.
Caught a glimpse of a bloke playing a tuba on the N68 last night. Only in Brixton would someone turn a bus ride into a jazz gig.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I heard a marching band practicing for Carnival in the park while the smell of grilled kebabs wafted from the street stalls.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain can you find a café that serves coffee brewed from beans ground in a 1920s vintage grinder. It's a time capsule in a cup.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a local by how they defend the taco truck on 24th while ignoring the line at La Taqueria. It's a whole vibe.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a group of tourists try to figure out the Muni map while right next to a driver playing the trumpet. Classic SF chaos in motion.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia's just about Gaudí and plazas, but the best vibe is in the quiet corners where the old bars still hum with regulars.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by their bewilderment at the Vermut rituals; they always look puzzled when locals cheerfully order their midday drink.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still use the Tube for everything. The locals know you can walk from Dalston Junction to most places faster than waiting for a train.
Saw a bloke on the 333 last night serenading a girl with a ukulele—she was trying to pretend she wasn’t listening but the whole bus was in on it.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw a kid riding a bike with a pet chicken in the basket down Sonnenallee. Only in Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only here do you find Späti owners refusing to sell you a beer after 2 AM, citing 'not tonight, too many drunkards'—it's oddly charming.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s local if they can name at least three taco trucks that definitely don’t take cards—no cash, no tacos.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 19 at 6 PM, no seats, but a guy juggling flaming torches while we rolled past city hall. Only in SF, right?

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists always miss how the locals really love their vermut hour; it’s not just a drink, it’s the social glue of the whole community.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always tell by the way they enthusiastically order a ‘café con leche’ and wait for it to arrive, like it’s a fancy drink.
